title: Telford / Wales: Collect Boys from Riding

main_route.excerpt: Camped in a small, sheltered site, green, with a pond at one end. * * * John & Charles very good. Supper at 9.pm.

Sat Picked up boys from riding, drove home listening to all their stories. Home by 2pm for lunch. On the day, we looked at "Four Winds", Grandma's & Grandpa's old house outside Telford — we had stopped for petrol just by their old road.
drove home listening to all their stories. Home by 2pm for lunch.

boundary_flags: mixed_start: page A_0111_R opens with the end of the previous trip before the Friday 13th Aug heading
summary: A short overnight trip on Friday 13–Saturday 14 August 1993, with Simon and Eve (and young sons John and Charles) driving from Birmingham to camp near the Shropshire/Wales border before collecting the older boys from a riding stay near Telford on Saturday and returning home by 2pm. En route home they paused to look at 'Four Winds', their grandparents' former house outside Telford.
extraction_notes: Page A_0111_R is a mixed-boundary page: the first paragraph describes the return home from the preceding trip; the new trip begins with the 'Friday 13th Aug' heading. The campsite is described but not named; geocoded as an approximate inferred_anchor in Shropshire/Welsh borders. The boys collected from 'riding' are likely Thomas, William, and/or Peter (the older children); John and Charles are explicitly mentioned as travelling with the parents. 'Four Winds' is Grandma and Grandpa's old house outside Telford — a brief visit while stopped for petrol, not an overnight stop. No crossings, no excursions.
